title: Living in Boulder vlog 4 - 16 - 2017: Rode 56 miles 4,500 feet

enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps a 56-mile, 4,500-foot bike ride from Boulder up to Nederland and Ward, Colorado. He talks about being high while riding, the scenic mountain roads, and his plan to keep riding until his weed runs out before starting new projects or website work in May.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: enjoys it and uses it as a reason to postpone work obligations
accepts he thinks less clearly and deliberately avoids website/work tasks until sober
finds it beautiful and highly recommends the ride
barely eating but still riding, assumes he's burning fat

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder, V-Log, for the April 16th, 2017.
Today, I had insomnia, really bad insomnia, last night.
Just laying there.
So I slept in today until like noon or something and got out the door at like 1.30.
Because I was really tired.
And got really stunned.
And like I am right now.
And then rode my bike up to Nederland.
I took the bike path as far as I could.
And then rode up across the P2P Catecourt Highway.
But when I got up to Nederland, I did do a video of Bark Reservoir and Eldora, which you can see.
And the ski resort still had snow on it.
And so I got to Nederland and then I went north towards Ward.
And I was climbing a lot.
And I noticed I should have taken the video or some photos.
But I saw a lot of snow right on the edge.
I just got a thought.
I was like, oh, I need to put thumbnails for my videos.
And also I could feel the oxygen.
I was extra weak.
And I remember how it was like that before when I'd ride.
I felt stronger when I was riding around the flats here than when I was out on the P2P Catecourt.
But P2P Catecourt was beautiful.
Because when you look to the west, to the left, there are those big mountains.
And at one point you're riding it and you see, you kind of feel like you're at the same level almost as a tree line.
So you feel like you're in the mountains really high.
And the road's in a really good shape.
Really smooth with a wide shoulder.
So I went to Ward and I did a video at Ward.
Ward's one of the old mine towns.
You can see in the middle of the video there is the church, the old church.
It's like over 100 years old.
So then I rode down and that was fun to do a downhill really fast for a long time.
And it was a long time.
You're going down fast for a long time.
And back into Boulder.
I think it was like 4,500 feet of climbing.
So I was thinking about running when I got back.
But I think what I'm going to do is only ride until I run out of weed.
Because I only have like a few more days of weed left and I'm not going to buy more.
And it's like, you know, I want to take advantage of the weed.
Just hang out and smoke weed.
Instead of be all, we'll try to do different things, you know.
And then once I sober up, I can start doing different things.
Yeah, I work on my figure out, finish tie-up loosens on my website and try to figure out the whole ad sense thing and different stuff.
So, yeah, so I'm not, and also I have a bargain with myself.
Like, if I can ride all day long, you know, and listen to music, then as long as I'm still smoking weed, that's all I do.
I don't have to, like, work on my website or anything.
Because I feel tired and can't really think as much.
Yeah, I'm like really, really beat.
And my appetite hasn't kicked in yet.
It's weird.
I eat the same amount.
I barely eat anything and I'm still riding.
So, I must be burning calories for my fat.
So, tomorrow I would like to go on another ride.
Because I take advantage of as much free time as I can before I start to work.
Beginning of May.
And I think this week that we have one place.
But I want to do the same thing.
I want to ride up to Ward, like the same road in reverse.
And then ride peak to peak over to, like, the same brain canyon, I think it's called, down to Lyons.
That'll be a good, another mountain day.
Or maybe I can make it all the way to Estes Park.
So, okay, that's enough vlogging for today.
Peace out.
